bundleOrchestration.purpose: Define how repository-aware agents load, apply, layer and trace prompt bundles for the ResearchOps platform.

bootstrap.rule: Before any repository-affecting response, read AGENTS.md, this orchestration file and the bundle registry.
Use docs/devops/ResearchOps-Bundle-Setup.zip as the authoritative bundle package.
Do not rely on chat memory, uploaded copies of bundles, or prior conversation state as the source of truth.
If the operating model cannot be loaded, stop repository-affecting work and report the missing source.

bundleRef.when: Frontend, HTML, CSS, accessibility, forms, components, content design or page design are in scope.
Cloudflare Workers, Pages, Wrangler, routes, bindings or deployment are in scope.
Airtable tables, records, linked records, formula filters or attachments are in scope.
Mural OAuth, workspaces, rooms, murals, widgets or sticky notes are in scope.

precedence.rule: GitHub Diamond governs repository safety, commits, branches, pull requests and CI.
ResearchOps Developer governs platform architecture and repository-specific implementation.
GOV.UK Design System governs GOV.UK UI, accessibility, interaction patterns and content patterns.
Platform API bundles govern API-specific implementation details where their domain is in scope.
When bundles conflict, record the conflict, apply the highest-precedence relevant rule and explain the impact.

traceability.rule: When [reasoning] is present, create an auditable trace.
The trace must record operating-model files loaded, bundles selected, bundles skipped, conflicts found and precedence decisions.
The trace must not expose private chain-of-thought.
